We tried all types of eateries in the medina and didn't have any issues, so it made us think that she was trying to sell us overpriced food from her. I thought we only had to deal with taxi drivers and merchants trying to overcharge us, not a property manager. I didn't like that.
Property reply: Thank you for your review. I am glad that you enjoyed your stay. Moving forward, my manager will use the Google Translate app. This was clearly a loss in translation. We never use sales tactics to pressure our guests into buying anything. My guests' well being is always sacred.

Positive review:

Property reply: Cher Yannis et Lise, cela fut un veritable plaisir de vous avoir avec nous. Merci infiniment pour votre gentillesse et votre generosite. A tres bientot.

I trust that when guests evaluate the riad, they will also read the other positive reviews. I trust in the Universe that there are good people who have discernement and not judge us on your exceptional and unfair poor reviews. Its unfortunate that you had a bad experience and I am sorry for this. I emailed you asking for your dietary requirements.  If you told me that you are lactose intolerant, we would have prepared a different breakfast. You clearly did not read the 2 emails I sent you. Communication is a two-way street. Now, I will clarify the truth about your accusations against Khadija. Your husband who speaks perfect French asked Khadija to arrange transportation to Agafay without mentioning that you were going to the Agafay Luxury Camp. Instead you waited until you arrived in Agafay to tell the driver what is the exact location you wanted to go. This camp is located at the end of the Agafay not at the beginning. Thats why the price quote is not the same. If you told them that you were going to that camp from the start, they would have given you the correct price. And it was up to you to decide. Also, from what the drivers told me, your husband was agressive and abusive to them. Khadija has never been abusive to anyone. This has been confirmed by the driver who was with you. Also just read her guest reviews. You were all screaming in the ca and I think that things got heated too much.  You can make me accountable for recommending you the driver but I am telling things as they are and there was clearly no malicious intent to scam you as you claim. If you got scammed throughout your whole trip by other people, it is unfair that you assume the worse from us. You cannot make defamatory allegations like this against someone who tried to help you out. Unlike other riads, we do not sell excursions to our guests. We only respond to guests requests if they need a driver. We do not take any commission. The price is quoted by the driver and offered to guests at cost price. So what is the benefit for Khadija to scam you in this event? Why would we jeopardize our reputation for 20 Euros? We are not bad people getting in life to scam people.  Khadija and the riad have 5.0 star reviews on booking and other platforms.  If this was the case, people would have flagged us a long time ago. I am sorry that you had to feel that you were scammed by everybody. If you want to see the bad in someone this is what you will see. And if you decide to see us as bad people, well it's your perogative but you do not have the right to accuse someone of wrong doing by assuming the worse and especially without proof. Next time, make sure that your communications are crystal clear before you go anywhere. That applies to Morocco and to any place in the world. I wish you well.

Dear Magdalena,
Thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts on your recent stay. We're delighted you enjoyed your time at the Riad! We apologize you had trouble finding us. Property reply:
Hi Lena,
Thank you for your kind review. We greatly appreciate it. That said, I will address your comment about the price of the dinners. We ask for 250 dh / person for a 3-course meal (salads, tagines, desserts). This is the price that all good quality riads charge in Marrakech and Fes. Please do a web search and you will see that this price is normal. Maybe some lower quality riads ask for less but we certainly do not claim to be the cheapest riad in Marrakech. We source our food from local farms. We make our breads and jams in-house. Most of our food are home made not store bought. Quality and value are important to us. We have no interest in price gouging our guests. And so, at the end of the day, I am happy that you found our breakfast delicious.

So quiet and peaceful even though right in the middle of the medina - beautifully decorated. Delicious homemade breakfast in the courtyard, and Khadija was a wonderful host. Greeted us with mint tea and biscottis and even left us some fresh pastries the night before an early flight.

It's incidents like this that people show their true colors and this event certainly showed what kind of inconsiderate people run this establishment.
Property reply: As a reminder, all cancellations after one day before check-in have a 100% penalty fee. If you have decided to leave the riad, it was your personal decision. The riad has not stopped operating and we have welcomed guests ever since. There is no travel advisory urging people to leave Marrakesh. In that case, cancellation policies stand and there are no refunds. Please check with your travel insurance. They may make an exception and provide you compensation for this Act of God event. Itтs unfortunate that you felt it necessary to leave us with totally untrue and unkind comments as retaliation. Kind regards.

Negative review:
The room I was assigned was in the Building next door, which means every time I wanted to go to the patio I needed to exit my building and enter the other one. The shower had air when I hopped in and there was no toilet brush.
More than anything, I agreed for a meeting point on the Riad's suggestion. I decided to just go and it took me another 30 minutes to find the correct building.
Property reply: Hi Flavia, Thank you for your comments. These will surely help us make our guest care even better. To plan our breakfast meals, we asked for your dietary preferences: gluten-free, vegan, etc. That said, our breakfast changes every day and is served in rotation based on the order of the guest's arrival. The other guests were given a different breakfast because they did not arrive on the same day as you; they were served the same breakfast you had on their first day. As mentioned in my communications, if a guest needs anything, he or she just has to ask. We quickly respond to all requests. If you asked us for a toilet brush, we would have provided you with one. As for the directions, I myself (the owner) actually went to meet you at the meeting point, but you were not there. Then we called you, and you did not answer your phone. We are on Google Maps, the address is clearly visible on Booking and was provided again in the email we sent to all guests. If a guest still cannot come on their own, we are there to provide directions. Communication is a two-way street. We did everything possible to help you.  If you got lost, its a bit much to expect that we should go and find you on the streets. You gave us a rating of 2.5 for our location and 5 for the value. We are centrally located, steps away from the souks and the Place Jemaa el Fnaa. This makes me wonder what rating you would have given to a riad that is located a 20-minute walk from the main square. And if you find a riad that provides such a copious and high-quality breakfast at the price you paid, please do let me know. We are in the hospitality business, and at Riad di Siena, we always strive to exceed our guests' expectations, but we also deserve to be treated fairly. When you were at the riad, at any given time, you never asked or said anything. I can understand that it's probably easier to complain after the fact, and I respect that. However, your inability to communicate properly about your needs and expectations does not make us inadequate hosts. I wish you well.

The room was good, but quiet bare, lacked storage space like maybe a wardrobe or a shelf for clothing. Also a bathroom shelf or a few hooks would be nice. Was quite bright in the night from people switching on the staircase light, so a curtain for the door windows recommended.
Property reply: Hello Harald, Thank you for your review. Please note that the riad where you stayed, is the Annex of Riad di Siena; it is easily accessible, located less than 3 m from the main door. When guests stay at the Annex, they also get a key to the main Riad to have breakfast in the courtyard but also enjoy its soothing vibes whenever they wish. The Annex has contemporary decor, but the building is quite old. It is a 300-year-old riad that was part of the Vizir Palace next door. When I renovated it, I decided to use contemporary decor to transform the space with more light and brightness. That said, we only use traditional materials such as beldi cement tiles and handmade textiles and ceramics. Everything in this riad has been handmade by local artisans, not purchased from a generic big box store. The breakfasts all guests enjoy are also home-made, and we only use fresh ingredients that we get from local organic farmers. We make them from scratch, not store-bought. For the price you paid for the room, you would typically get a hostel room without an ensuite bathroomтmost probably at half the size of the room you hadтand a rudimentary breakfast. I understand that the facilities and amenities were not to your full satisfaction, but everything we listed on Booking is what guests will get at our riad - not less, not more. If guests expect more, they should look for a riad with more amenities and facilities. We do not pretend to be something that we are not. Riad di Siena is a beautiful but simple Riad with an emphasis on the human qualities of our team and the exceptional food we serve. That said, thank you for your suggestions. We always do our best to continually improve our offerings. Best wishes to you.

The electricity stopped twice when we tried to use the AC to get warm and dry out the air... When the electricity stopped we also didnt have WiFi of course which made it impossible to contact anyone for help... Thank god I went outside the room to find and turn on the switch by myself in the middle of the night, otherwise we would have stayed without light and any contact all night. The breakfast was same every morning and since my partner doesn't eat eggs we requested for some alternative; The second day my message wasn't even answered and on the last day when we clearly requested that to two people, guess what - we again received an omelette... Really disappointed tbh since we travelled for a special occasion and the expectations were higher due to the 9/10 rating that this place has, but it is nowhere near than a 6 maybe... You have to be really needing the basics like a rooftop over you to be fully satisfied.
Property reply:
Dear Methos,
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback about your recent stay with us. While we are always thrilled to receive positive feedback, we appreciate your honest thoughts, even when they highlight areas where we can improve.
We understand your concerns regarding the room and apologize if it did not fully meet your expectations. Please allow us to address your specific points:
Room ventilation: The door includes a window for air circulation. Perhaps you were not aware of this during your stay? We shall ensure it is clearly communicated to future guests.
Electricity: We regularly check the electricity, and, unfortunately, you did not mention this during your stay. We always fix issues as soon as guests tell us.
Towel drying: We apologize if the current towel drying arrangement was not readily apparent.
Room brightness: Feedback on lighting can be subjective. While another guest found the same room too bright, we understand that you found it too dark for your preferences.
Breakfast preferences: We offer daily menu variations and provide customized meals to guests who have allergies and specific dietary preferences. Based on our investigation, we did not find any record of your request to Khadija. All requests must be made to Khadija, not to the kitchen staff.
Room cleaning: Daily cleaning is performed for each room. We apologize if this was not to your satisfaction.
Communication: We strive for prompt responses to guest inquiries. While nighttime hours may limit immediate responses, we appreciate your understanding.
We value open communication and strongly encourage guests to share any concerns during their stay, allowing us to address them in real time. Your feedback helps us improve and continuously enhance the guest experience.
While we understand your disappointment, we believe our extensive positive reviews from other guests reflect our ongoing commitment to excellence. We strive to consistently exceed expectations, and your feedback helps us identify areas for improvement.
Your comments on our rating. I was saddened by your reviewsтto be honest, they were very harsh and your comment about our rating is unfair. Every guest has their own experience, and they all have every right to give us a high rating if they feel that we deserve itтand we do. We do not buy fake reviews. All reviews are genuine. So we definitely deserve the 9+ that we have on booking. And you can read all our other reviews on riaddisiena. Being highly rated by more than 500 guests is not just a pure coincidence. In any case, I am sad that you did not enjoy your experience, but I am convinced that if you reached out to me during your stay, I could have made things right on the spot. Thank you for your feedback. Much love and light.

You paid less than 50 euros for the room but you are showing the disappointment of someone who has paid 500 euros and got themselves bamboozled. If you don't know how to appreciate the imperfections of a traditional guest house, the many benefits that our riad offers, and you have unclear expectations, you should have stayed at a modern hotel that would have better met your needs. When you arrived on the first day and were unhappy with the room, we offered you the opportunity to cancel without any penalty fee. You declined and insisted on staying. But unfortunately, you stayed, and now I have to address your long list of complaints.
First, you didn't take the time to read all the communications I sent you before your arrival, especially the part about informing us in advance of any allergies. You were also in contact with our riad manager before your arrival. Did you think of letting her know of your specific needs? You only mentioned your allergies when you came for breakfast. Did you think we have easy access to gluten-free bread like in Europe or a large kitchen staff to whip up gluten-free pastries on short notice? Despite this, we still bought gluten-free bread for you for the following days, which, of course, you failed to mention. You are not children; you should know what you can eat, especially if you have allergies. Why suggest that we deliberately caused your food poisoning because we тrefusedт to give you gluten-free bread?
Second, your claims about dust and cleaning are completely false, hurtful, and defamatory. The room and textiles were not dirty. You should educate yourself about the configuration of a riad as well as the imperfections of vintage handmade rugs, textiles, and all Moroccan handicrafts. Do you know what a vintage Moroccan rug looks like? Yes, to uneducated people, they might look dirty, but they are not. Handmade cement tiles in Morocco can appear imperfect and, unfortunately, absorb stains. We cleaned the room every day during your stay. Even if we cleaned your room every half hour, you would have found something to complain about. If the room was truly that dirty, don't you think other guests would have flagged it? Imagine this: for the past year, many "clueless" guests have stayed in our "dirty" room and slept on a "filthy" bed without knowing it! You are the first person who saw these horrible things and saved us all!! It's unfortunate that you didn't like your room, but that doesn't give you the right to accuse us of dishonesty and bad intentions, or to exaggerate and invent problems that didn't exist.
Every flaw you mentioned, like humidity or rooms with no windows, are common in all riads in the Medina. There are even other issues that you did not have to experience with us: very small rooms with limited space, smelly drains, no water pressure, no hot water, non-functioning AC, no wifi signals, a 20-minute walk from the main square, constant noise, no privacy, sweet breakfast options, rude staff, bad neighborhood, etc. I'm glad you didn't mention these because you did not experience them at our riad, but you might in other riads.
To help our guests understand local life in the Medina and how we operate, we are meticulously honest in our communications; we donтt withhold anything. Our riad has a 100% occupancy rate, and we don't force anyone to choose us. Claiming that we hide information on purpose is simply disingenuous. Work on your negative point of view when you visit a country with a different culture. You seem to expect the worst in people and situations. Did any animals come into your room during your stay? Do you think an insect or a mouse needs a door to enter a room? The doors of the room are antiques, almost 100 years old. Removing them would be a shame. What's the point of focusing on all the what-ifs? When people focus on the negative, that's what they will see. So, if you have decided that we are the worst, that's your prerogative, and it only reflects on you. And remember. We gave you the opportunity to run. You could have switched hotel on the first day. On the second day. On the third day. But you decided to stay in the тfilthт. I personally would not if a room is that dirty. As a first time visitor of Marrakech, reading my messages would have helped you understand the challenges of staying in the Medina. Or chose another accommodation. Next time, considering your high expectations and mindset, book a modern hotel with luxurious amenities outside of the Medina.

Property reply: Please look at your invoice on Booking. It is clearly indicated that you needed to pay the city taxes of 50 dh (5 euros). City taxes in Marrakech are 2.5 euros per person per night. Nous sommes toujours ouverts aux retours constructifs et avons un profond respect pour chacun de nos clients. Cependant, nous avons УЉgalement des rУЈgles claires, mises en place pour assurer le confort, la sУЉcuritУЉ et le bien-УЊtre de tous nos invitУЉs.

La gentillesse fait partie intУЉgrante de nos valeurs. Mais la gentillesse ne signifie pas faiblesse. Review title: Nice stay near the town center
Positive review:
Staff: Great communication with Zahra. A very polite and helpful lady.
Room: The room is not something luxury (as already mentioned in the decsription) but it was nice and cozy.
Breakfast: Good and handmade. Each day, something different was prepared by the lady in the kitchen.

Negative review:
Room: the room was located on the ground floor. During our stay, there was a heavy downpour, which caused the riad's courtyard to flood and the water to enter the room. We were out of the room at the time. The staff's actions were immediate (they opened the large manhole to drain the water, and cleaned the room again).
From the smell of humidity that was already present in the room, I understand that this was not the first time something like this had happened.
Property reply:
Thank you for your feedback. Weтre glad you acknowledged Zahraтs care. That said, weтd like to address several points raised in your review.

You expressed disappointment that the room was тnot something luxury.т To be clearтwe never described it as such. Nowhere in our listing, on our website, or in our guest communication do we use the word luxury. We are deliberate with language. We describe The Riad as a sanctuary. We speak of care, simplicity done well, and a lived-in presence. But never luxury.

And yet, that is the paradox:
We are being benchmarked against a standard we never claimed.
We clearly communicated that this space is rooted in simplicity and authenticity.
Still, we are judged for not offering what we never promised to begin with.

Itтs important not to confuse sacred care with hotel luxury. They are not the same. What we offer is not curated through branding or polishтit is lived, handmade, and intentional. We do not offer high-gloss surfaces or boutique theatrics. We offer presence, rhythm, and quiet detail.

To guests unfamiliar with this kind of offering, care may be misread as luxury. But that is a misunderstanding of tone, not a failure of service.

We are not here to impress.
We are here to hold space.
For those seeking something real, that is more than enough.

And real is not shiny. Not polished. Not perfection. And not luxury.

т

You received clear, step-by-step arrival instructions, including live WhatsApp support. We remained in contact throughout your stay. All you had to do was ask.

Regarding our location: The Riad is less than two minutes on foot from Place Jemaa el Fna. We are not hidden in the deep maze of the medina. We are central, accessible, and safe.

As for the humidity: this is common in traditional riads, particularly in the warmer months. These homes are built around enclosed courtyards that regulate temperature, but naturally limit airflow. This is not unique to our houseтit is part of medina architecture. We communicated this clearly before arrival.

We have taken steps to mitigate it, including installing windows in doors and providing air conditioning in all rooms. But this is still a traditional homeтnot a sealed, climate-controlled hotel.

The flash flood on Saturday was a rare and extreme weather event. Zahra responded immediately and resolved the situation with care and speed.

We wish you clarity and ease in your future travels.

Negative review: The rooms smell mold.
Property reply:
Thank you for your feedback. Just to clarify: there is no mold in the rooms. Some traditional Moroccan rooms can feel humid due to their architecture. This is explained clearly in our two pre-arrival messages, along with the simple solutionтopen the window in the door and use the AC. Itтs unfortunate that this information was missed.

Weтre glad you appreciated the location and our care, though the harsh score does not reflect the kindness and generosity you received. It seems the room did not meet your preconceived expectations, and that outweighed the human care offered.

Our house is not for everyoneтand thatтs okay. We hope to host guests who look beyond the expectation for тluxuryт and тperfectionт and begin to appreciate her for what she truly is. And we are grateful for the many guests who do.

To future guests: please read our pre-arrival messages. We never pretend to be what we are not.

Wishing you all the best on your future travels.

Thank you for taking the time to share your impressions.

I hear your concerns, and I understand that cleanliness is a core priority for you. We are a small, lived-in riadтnot a hotelтand while we strive for warmth, care, and attentiveness, we do not offer standardized daily housekeeping. Our cleaning protocols are intentionally simple and transparent, as stated in the listing and reinforced during our communication prior to your stay.

When you requested bathroom photos before booking, I gently suggested that if perfection in that area was essential, you might be happier elsewhere. That advice still stands. We do not pretend to be what we are not. We offer a soulful, real environmentтone that values beauty, authenticity, and human presence over sterile polish.

Your feedback about the rugs is noted. However, these are traditional Moroccan rugsтwoven by hand, large, and heavy. They require professional cleaning and take several days to dry. It is not realistic, nor common practice in Morocco, to wash them between each guest. Hotels, by contrast, typically use wall-to-wall synthetic carpeting or washable textiles for precisely this reason. A traditional riad with vintage pieces carries a different rhythmтand different responsibilities.

Not every space is for everyone. And thatтs okay. I wish you well on your continued travelsтand hope you find the kind of perfection that brings you peace.

Property reply:
We are located a 2-minute walk from Jemaa el Fna and the Koutoubia Mosque т the very heart of Marrakech.

When you decided to give us a 2.0 for location and called it тvery hidden,т it likely means you didnтt read the step-by-step arrival directions (with photos) we send in advance т and you may not fully understand how the medina is structured.

All riads in Marrakech are тhidden.т Thatтs the nature of life in the medina.
Did you see any riads lining the main streets or in the middle of the souks?
Did you have to walk 20 minutes to the main square? Thatтs the standard.

Ours is one of the most accessible riads in the city.

To future guests: please read the pre-arrival messages.
They contain everything you need to find us easily and know what to expect.
We also offer live WhatsApp support т if you get lost or need anything, just ask.
Giving us a low rating because you chose not to read or ask for help is frustrating т and unfair.

We are present, responsive, and clear.

Low ratings for location or comfort usually come from guests who arrive unprepared and expect a version of luxury that has nothing to do with what we actually offer. Our pre-arrival messages explain it all т what we provide, and what we donтt.

People who seek a feeling of home and heart-based service love us.
People who seek performative luxury often donтt т and tend to leave mediocre reviews for тproblemsт that arenтt actually problems.

Know the difference.

If you gave us a 4.0 because the room was dirty, there was no hot water, or the staff was rude т that would be fair. But those things didnтt happen.

We canтt т and wonтt т cater to imagined expectations.
Read the listing. Read the pre-arrival messages.
Rate us on what we actually offer т not what you assumed we might.

And if something is missing, tell us. Thatтs how reviews help us grow.

Reviews are meant to reward what a business does well т not punish it for what it never claimed to offer. We go above and beyond т and guests who read, ask, and engage always recognize this.

But when reviews reflect misaligned expectations rather than actual experience, they say more about the reviewer than the house.

If you asked for something and we failed to deliver, a 4.0 is fair.
But if you said in person that everything was wonderful т and later gave us a low score from behind a screen т thatтs not feedback. Thatтs avoidance.

Reviews are not a game. They carry real consequences.
When used to punish rather than reflect, thatтs a misuse of power.

If you expected a modern Western locking system, you can find them at standard hotels.

If by тit could be betterт you mean new fittings and a modern, sanitized and flawless hotel style, that is not who we are, and this is made clear in our pre-arrival messages and on our website. However, guests who are are pregnant, have  back pain or other sensitive sleep requirements may experience discomfort in traditional riads or any other accommodation, which do not offer orthopedic bedding, particularly at budget price points.

This is not a defect of what we provide but a matter of personal needs that are typically met only in modern or luxury hotels.

For this reason, we encourage future guests with specific health or sleep requirements to communicate them in advance, so expectations can be aligned appropriately. We prefer to decline a booking rather than set expectations we cannot meet.

This creates a disconnect. A 6.0 does not help us identify areas for improvement, nor does it clearly inform future guests what was lacking, especially when the written feedback is positive.

While personal taste and preferences naturally vary, we believe that care, service, and the overall experience should be reflected proportionately in the overall rating.

We communicate clearly what our house is and what it is not, both in our listing and in our pre-arrival messages. It is fair to evaluate the stay based on what we state we provide, rather than on expectations we do not claim to offer.

Property reply:
Dear Patricia,

Thank you for taking the time to share your experience and for your generous words about Zahra. She carries the house with care and consistency, and your recognition of her work is well appreciated.

Regarding the room: We do not assign rooms randomly. We assigned the room that you reserved. The exact room category, photographs, and description are visible prior to confirmation, and our pre-arrival messages clearly explain the architectural realities of a traditional riad т including humidity, interior rooms, and the nature of Medina construction. This house is centuries old; it is not a modern concrete hotel.

There is a door separating the bathroom from the bedroom. During your stay, you broke the bathroom door and the toilet seat and they were fixed immediately. When technical issues arise, they are addressed without delay.

Humidity can occur in historic riads, particularly in interior rooms, and this is precisely why air conditioning and a dehumidifier are provided. When in use, they effectively regulate the environment.

When Zahra checked in with you during your stay, you confirmed that everything was perfect. Had any discomfort been raised at that time, we would have gladly assisted further.

As for the bedding, the configuration reflects the room category booked. Adjusting layouts impacts inventory management for future guests and cannot be changed on a case-by-case basis.

It is important to distinguish between the inherent characteristics of a centuries-old riad and service shortcomings. Many guests choose this experience specifically for its authenticity; it may not suit everyone.

Clarity before arrival prevents disappointment. We encourage all guests to read the 2 pre-arrival messages we send. Reading them prevents misalignment. 

We wish you well.

Property reply:
Thank you for your review, Patrizia. We are glad you appreciated the location.
We want to address your comment about Zahra directly, because honesty matters more to us than polished replies. Zahra has been personally named and praised in the reviews of hundreds of guests т for her kindness, her attentiveness, and her warmth. That is not an accident. That is who she is, every single day.
What we would gently ask you to consider is context. Your stay fell during Ramadan т the holiest month in the Islamic calendar т when Zahra, like millions of Moroccans, was fasting from sunrise to sunset. No food, no water, while still preparing your breakfast, attending to your needs, and caring for you with the same devotion she gives every guest. When you travel to Morocco, you are entering a Muslim country with its own rhythms and sacred traditions. You do not need to observe Ramadan yourself, but awareness and respect go a long way.
She cared for you throughout your stay. If her energy during a month of fasting felt different to you, that is an invitation to understand something new about the culture you chose to visit т not a reason to mark someone down.
We wish you well on your future travels.

Nuestra estancia en este riad en Marrakech fue, en general, una experiencia muy agradable, marcada sobre todo por el trato excepcional recibido. Queremos destacar especialmente a Zhara, cuya hospitalidad hizo que nos sintiУЉramos como en casa desde el primer momento. Siempre atenta, amable y dispuesta a ayudar, nos ofreciУГ recomendaciones muy УКtiles y estuvo pendiente de cada detalle durante nuestra estancia.
El desayuno fue otro de los puntos fuertes: variado, abundante y delicioso, perfecto para empezar el dУ­a con energУ­a antes de recorrer la ciudad.
Zhara siempre estaba atentos a nosotros.
Sin dudar alguna para nosotros es un punto fuerte para el Riad.
AdemУЁs, valoramos mucho toda la ayuda que nos brindaron durante nuestra visita, facilitУЁndonos informaciУГn y haciendo que todo fuera mucho mУЁs sencillo y cУГmodo.
Como punto a mejorar, nuestra habitaciУГn no contaba con ventana, lo que hacУ­a que el espacio se sintiera algo cerrado. TambiУЉn notamos un poco de humedad, algo que creemos podrУ­a solucionarse para hacer la estancia aУКn mУЁs confortable.
En resumen, un lugar con un trato humano excelente y detalles muy cuidados, que con pequeУБas mejoras en las habitaciones podrУ­a ofrecer una experiencia perfecta.
Property reply:
Dear Tamara,

Thank you for such a thoughtful and generous review. We are proud that Zahra's care made your stay feel like home т she will be very happy to read your words. We are glad the breakfast set the right tone each morning.

Regarding the room: traditional older Medina riads  - like our 300-year old riad - were built with interior-facing rooms and no exterior windows. This is not a flaw or an oversight т it is how these houses were designed, for cooling and protection. Natural humidity is also part of life in a centuries-old earthen structure. We provide air conditioning and door windows to support comfort, but the architecture itself is not something we can or wish to change. It is the house as it was built.

Both of these details are addressed in the two pre-arrival messages we send to every guest before check-in, alongside a full description of the house, its structure, and what to expect. We encourage future guests to read them carefully т they are written to prevent exactly this kind of misunderstanding.

We wish you both wonderful travels ahead.

Property reply:
This reservation was cancelled and fully refunded at your request. Since you mentioned that an overnight stay was not possible, why the comment that we pushed you to leave? Did you expect to stay despite the "huge" problem? This is how it usually works: when a booking is cancelled and refunded in full, the room is no longer yours.

We were not obligated to refund. We showed goodwill and refunded, no questions asked. Your decision to write unsubstantiated claims on a refunded reservation and voided contract shows a misplaced desire to "warn" others or to be proven right.

We transparently explain in all our communications why century-old houses have humidity. We do not hide anything. Whether a riad in Morocco, a palazzo in Sicily, or a townhouse in New Orleans, there is humidity т especially when the current Marrakech weather is heavy with clouds and storms.

But you looked for mold. You found none. Because there is none. Biology 101: there is no way "extreme" mold does not show, even if the room is freshly painted every week т our last paint work was in January. Yet, even with no proof, you assumed guilt and bad intentions on our end.

Thatтs not a review. Thatтs not a genuine desire to help. Thatтs misdirected retaliation.

As expected, guests before and after this specific case completed their stay without any complaints.
